title: thymelaea thesioides subsp. elliptica
intro: Thymelaea thesioides subsp. elliptica, also known as the spiny broom or black thorn, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its antidiarrheal and anti-inflammatory properties. The spiny broom has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion by boiling its leaves, stems, or roots, and is also used in herbal preparations such as salves, ointments, and poultices.
